description_long: This is a short wooden fence section composed of three vertical posts and a single horizontal rail. The wood appears weathered, with visible grain and some darkened areas suggesting exposure to the elements. The tops of the vertical posts are cut at an angle, creating an alternating height pattern along the top edge. Four metallic fasteners are visible on the front face of the horizontal rail, securing the posts in place. The construction is simple and utilitarian, with no decorative elements or embellishments. This type of fence is commonly used in urban parks, gardens, or as a boundary marker along sidewalks.
description: A short, weathered wooden fence with three angled posts and a single horizontal rail, secured by visible metal fasteners.
description_view_0: Front view showing three vertical wooden posts, a single horizontal rail, and four metal fasteners.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the wood and the staggered heights of the post tops.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the same three posts and rail but less detail on the fasteners.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and angled tops of the posts.
